title: Create job requisitions
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to create job requisitions capturing full role details, so that open positions are formally captured and can move through the hiring pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have hiring/requisition permissions, when I create a new requisition, then the system saves it with position details (title, department, location, headcount, job description, requirements) and an initial status; Given required fields are missing, when I attempt to save, then the system blocks the save and flags the missing mandatory fields; Given a requisition is saved, when I view the requisition list, then the new requisition appears with its current status; Given a draft requisition, when I submit it, then it enters the approval workflow and its status changes to reflect submission

title: Requisition approval workflow
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to review and approve or reject submitted job requisitions through a defined approval workflow, so that only authorized positions proceed to sourcing.
acceptance_criteria: Given a requisition is submitted for approval, when it reaches me as the designated approver, then I can approve or reject it and add comments/justification; Given multiple approval levels are configured, when one level approves, then the requisition routes to the next approver until fully approved; Given I approve a requisition, when the decision is recorded, then its status updates to approved and it becomes eligible for sourcing; Given I reject a requisition, when the decision is recorded, then its status updates to rejected/returned and the originator is notified; Given any approval action occurs, when it is saved, then the approver, decision, timestamp, and comments are captured in the audit trail

title: Track requisition status
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to track the status of each job requisition across the pipeline, so that I always know where every open position stands.
acceptance_criteria: Given active requisitions exist, when I open the requisition dashboard, then each requisition shows its current status (e.g., draft, pending approval, approved, sourcing, on hold, filled, closed, cancelled); Given a requisition progresses, when its stage changes, then the status is updated accordingly and the change is timestamped; Given I need to focus, when I filter or sort requisitions by status, department, recruiter, or hiring manager, then the list reflects the applied criteria; Given a requisition is filled or cancelled, when its status is set, then it is excluded from active-open reporting

title: Assign requisitions to recruiters and hiring managers
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign each requisition to a recruiter and a hiring manager, so that ownership of every hiring effort is clearly defined.
acceptance_criteria: Given an approved requisition, when I assign a recruiter and a hiring manager, then the assignments are saved against the requisition; Given an assignment exists, when the assignee views their workload, then the assigned requisition appears in their queue; Given ownership must change, when I reassign the recruiter or hiring manager, then the previous assignment is replaced and both parties are notified; Given a requisition has no assignee, when it is approved for sourcing, then the system flags it as unassigned for action
source: FR 6.13.1 (Job Requisitions — assignment to recruiters and hiring managers)

title: Source and manage candidates in the talent pool
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to source candidates into a talent pool, so that potential applicants are captured and available for open requisitions.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am sourcing candidates, when I add a candidate to the talent pool, then the candidate record (contact details, profile, source channel) is stored and retrievable; Given candidates exist in the pool, when I search or filter the pool by skills, role, status, or source, then matching candidates are returned; Given a candidate matches an open requisition, when I associate them, then the candidate is linked to that requisition; Given a duplicate candidate profile is added, when the system detects matching identifiers, then it warns of the potential duplicate

title: Track candidate applications and resumes
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to track candidate applications, store resumes, and update application status, so that I can manage each applicant's progress against a requisition.
acceptance_criteria: Given a candidate applies, when I record the application, then it is linked to the candidate and the requisition with the resume/document stored and retrievable; Given an application is in progress, when I update its status (e.g., applied, screened, shortlisted, interviewing, offered, hired, rejected), then the new status is reflected in the application tracking view and timestamped; Given multiple applications exist, when I view a requisition, then all applicants and their current stages are visible; Given a resume is uploaded, when I open the application, then the stored resume can be viewed or downloaded
source: FR 6.13.2 (Talent Pool Management — application tracking, resume storage, status tracking)

title: Define interview panels
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define interview panels for a requisition, so that the right evaluators are assigned to assess candidates.
acceptance_criteria: Given a requisition needs interviews, when I define an interview panel, then the selected panel members are saved for that hiring process; Given a panel is defined, when scheduling an interview, then panel members are available to be selected; Given a panel member must change, when I update the panel, then the revised membership is saved and reflected in scheduling; Given interview rounds are configured, when I assign panel members per round, then each round retains its own panel composition

title: Schedule interviews with calendar integration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to schedule interviews with calendar integration, so that candidates and panel members have coordinated interview times.
acceptance_criteria: Given a panel and candidate are ready, when I schedule an interview, then the interview is booked and reflected via calendar integration for all participants; Given an interview is scheduled, when panel members check their calendar, then the interview appears at the scheduled time with candidate and role details; Given a scheduling conflict exists for a panel member, when I attempt to book, then the system surfaces the conflict; Given an interview is rescheduled or cancelled, when I save the change, then calendar entries and participant notifications are updated accordingly
source: FR 6.13.3 (Interview Management — scheduling with calendar integration)

title: Evaluate candidates with structured scorecards
user_story: As an Employee (User) serving on an interview panel, I want to record evaluations using structured scorecards with configured criteria, so that candidate assessments are consistent and comparable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on an interview panel, when I complete a scorecard, then my evaluation is captured against the configured evaluation criteria with ratings and comments; Given scorecard criteria are configured, when I open the scorecard, then it presents exactly those criteria; Given I submit a scorecard, when it is saved, then it is attributed to me, timestamped, and linked to the candidate and interview round; Given multiple panel members evaluate a candidate, when scores are viewed, then evaluations are comparable side by side to support the hiring decision
source: FR 6.13.3 (Interview Management — structured scorecards with configurable evaluation criteria)

title: Track reference checks
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to track and document reference feedback for candidates, so that reference outcomes are recorded as part of the hiring decision.
acceptance_criteria: Given a candidate is in the hiring process, when I record reference feedback, then the feedback (referee details, questions, responses, outcome) is documented against the candidate; Given multiple references are required, when I log each one, then all reference records are tracked with their completion status; Given reference checks are recorded, when I review the candidate, then the reference feedback is visible to authorized users; Given reference checks are pending, when I view the candidate, then outstanding references are flagged as incomplete
source: FR 6.13.4 (Reference Checks — tracking and documentation of reference feedback)

title: Generate offers from templates and route for approval
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to generate offer letters from configurable templates and route them through approval, so that offers are consistent and authorized before distribution.
acceptance_criteria: Given a candidate is selected, when I generate an offer from a configurable template, then the offer letter is produced using that template with candidate/role/compensation details merged in; Given an offer requires approval, when it is submitted, then it follows the configured approval workflow before it can be sent; Given an approver acts, when they approve or reject the offer, then the decision, approver, and timestamp are recorded and the offer status updates; Given an offer is rejected in approval, when returned, then it cannot be distributed until revised and re-approved
source: FR 6.13.5 (Offer Management — configurable templates, approval workflows)

title: Receive offers electronically and record response
user_story: As an Employee (User) who is a candidate, I want to receive an offer electronically and accept or reject it, so that my hiring decision is recorded promptly.
acceptance_criteria: Given an approved offer, when it is distributed electronically, then I receive the offer and can view the offer letter; Given I respond to the offer, when I accept or reject it, then the acceptance/rejection is tracked in the system with a timestamp; Given I accept an offer, when the acceptance is recorded, then the candidate becomes eligible for conversion to employee; Given an offer has a response deadline, when the deadline passes without a response, then the system flags the offer as expired/pending follow-up
source: FR 6.13.5 (Offer Management — acceptance/rejection tracking, electronic distribution)

title: Run end-to-end hiring workflow
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a comprehensive hiring workflow that carries a candidate from requisition through sourcing, screening, interviews, reference checks, and offer, so that hiring progresses in an orderly, trackable sequence.
acceptance_criteria: Given a hiring effort, when a candidate moves through requisition, sourcing, screening, interviews, reference checks, and offer, then the workflow tracks each stage in sequence with status and ownership; Given a stage has entry prerequisites, when the prior stage is incomplete, then the workflow prevents skipping ahead or flags the gap; Given a candidate is rejected at any stage, when the decision is recorded, then the workflow closes the candidate with a reason; Given the pipeline is active, when I view the workflow, then the current stage of each candidate is visible end to end
source: FR 6.13.6 (Hiring Workflows — end-to-end requisition to offer)

title: Convert accepted candidate to employee with onboarding handoff
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to convert a candidate who accepts an offer into an employee with a seamless handoff to onboarding, so that hiring flows into onboarding without re-entering data.
acceptance_criteria: Given a candidate has accepted an offer, when I convert them to an employee, then their captured information (personal, role, compensation, documents) is handed off to the onboarding module without re-entry; Given conversion occurs, when the employee record is created, then the source requisition is marked filled and the candidate status updates to hired; Given required onboarding data is missing, when I attempt conversion, then the system flags the gaps before completing the handoff; Given conversion completes, when I check the onboarding module, then the new hire appears ready for onboarding
source: FR 6.13.7 (Candidate Conversion — seamless handoff to onboarding); FR 6.13.6 (Hiring Workflows — conversion to employee)

title: Configure offer letter templates
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure and maintain offer letter templates, so that offers are generated consistently with the correct content and merge fields.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage offer configuration, when I create or edit an offer letter template, then the template with its content and merge fields is saved for use; Given a template exists, when an offer is generated, then the selected template's content and merge fields populate the offer; Given a template is deactivated, when generating an offer, then the inactive template is not selectable; Given template changes are made, when saved, then existing generated offers retain the version they were created from
source: FR 6.13.5 (Offer Management — configurable offer letter templates)

title: Configure interview scorecard evaluation criteria
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure the scorecard evaluation criteria, so that interview assessments are standardized and comparable across candidates.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage interview configuration, when I define or edit scorecard evaluation criteria (criteria, rating scales, weightings), then they are saved for use in scorecards; Given criteria are configured, when a panel member opens a scorecard, then it presents exactly those criteria and scales; Given evaluation criteria need to change, when I update the scorecard configuration, then subsequent interviews use the updated criteria while completed scorecards retain the criteria in effect at the time

title: Screen candidates and make hiring recommendations as hiring manager
user_story: As an Employee (User) acting as the hiring manager, I want to review applicants, resumes, and panel feedback for my requisitions, so that I can screen candidates and make informed hiring decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am the assigned hiring manager, when I open my requisition, then I can view applicants, their resumes, application status, and interview scorecards; Given I evaluate a candidate, when I shortlist, advance, or reject them, then the candidate's application status updates accordingly; Given interview feedback is submitted, when I review the candidate, then consolidated panel evaluations and reference feedback are visible to support my recommendation; Given I recommend an offer, when I submit the recommendation, then it feeds into the offer generation and approval workflow
source: FR 6.13.1, FR 6.13.6 (Hiring Workflows — screening; hiring manager assignment)

title: Convert candidate to a non-user employee record
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to convert a hired candidate into an Employee (Non-User) record when they will not have system access, so that their employment data is captured for onboarding and HR without provisioning a login.
acceptance_criteria: Given a hired candidate will not require system access, when I convert them, then an Employee (Non-User) record is created and handed off to onboarding without login provisioning; Given the non-user employee record is created, when HR views the roster, then the record appears with employment details but no active user account; Given the candidate later requires access, when their status is upgraded, then a user account can be provisioned from the existing record
source: FR 6.13.7 (Candidate Conversion — conversion to employee record)

title: Standardize talent acquisition policies across group companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to define standardized requisition approval rules, offer templates, and evaluation criteria across the group's companies, so that hiring practices are consistent and governed centrally.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer a group of companies, when I define group-level standards for requisition approval, offer templates, and scorecard criteria, then they are applied or made available to the companies in the group; Given company-level overrides are permitted, when a Company Admin customizes within allowed limits, then the override is retained without breaching group standards; Given I review hiring across the group, when I open group-level talent acquisition reporting, then requisition, pipeline, and offer metrics are visible across all group companies
source: FR 6.13.1, FR 6.13.3, FR 6.13.5 (governance across group companies)

title: Oversee hiring activity across the portfolio
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want cross-company visibility into talent acquisition activity and metrics, so that I can oversee hiring performance across the portfolio of companies I manage.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer a portfolio of companies, when I open portfolio talent acquisition reporting, then I can view aggregated requisition volumes, pipeline stages, time-to-hire, and offer acceptance across companies; Given I need detail, when I drill into a specific company, then that company's requisitions and hiring pipeline are visible read-only; Given data spans multiple companies, when I filter by company, department, or period, then the metrics reflect the applied criteria

title: Provision and configure the Talent Acquisition module
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to enable and configure the Talent Acquisition module and its integrations for tenants, so that companies can run hiring workflows with the required capabilities available.
acceptance_criteria: Given a tenant requires talent acquisition, when I enable the module for that tenant, then requisitions, talent pool, interview, offer, and conversion capabilities become available to authorized roles; Given calendar and onboarding integrations are needed, when I configure them at the platform level, then interview scheduling and candidate conversion handoff function for tenants; Given the module is disabled for a tenant, when users access it, then talent acquisition features are not available; Given platform configuration changes, when saved, then they are applied per tenant and captured in the audit log
source: FR 6.13 (module provisioning; calendar and onboarding integration enablement)

title: Maintain effective-dated history of requisition and candidate records
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want every requisition, candidate, application, and offer record to retain a complete effective-dated history, so that I can reconstruct the state of any hiring artifact as of any point in time.
acceptance_criteria: Given a requisition or candidate record is changed, when the update is saved, then the prior version is preserved with valid-from/valid-to effective dates rather than overwritten; Given I need to audit a hiring decision, when I view a record's history, then I can see who changed what and when across all status and field changes; Given a record is queried as-of a past date, when I request the historical view, then the system returns the values that were in effect at that date; Given corrections are applied retroactively, when I amend a record, then both the corrected value and the original are retained in the bitemporal history
source: FR 6.13.1, FR 6.13.2 (L1 domain/data — bitemporal history & audit)

title: Enforce tenant-scoped isolation of talent acquisition data
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want all requisition, candidate, resume, and offer data to be strictly tenant-scoped with row-level security, so that no company can access another tenant's hiring data.
acceptance_criteria: Given multiple tenants share the platform, when a user queries requisitions or candidates, then row-level security returns only records belonging to that user's company/tenant; Given candidate PII and resumes are stored, when access is attempted, then storage enforces tenant scope and authorized-role checks before returning any document; Given a cross-tenant request is attempted through the API, when it is evaluated, then it is denied and the attempt is logged; Given a company is offboarded, when its tenant scope is disabled, then its talent acquisition data is no longer accessible to any active session

title: Enforce uniqueness and referential integrity across hiring records
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the data model to enforce uniqueness and referential integrity across requisitions, candidates, applications, and offers, so that hiring data stays consistent and free of orphaned or duplicate records.
acceptance_criteria: Given a candidate is created, when matching unique identifiers (email/phone) already exist in the tenant, then the system enforces the uniqueness constraint and prevents a silent duplicate; Given an application references a requisition and a candidate, when it is saved, then referential integrity ensures both parents exist and are within the same tenant; Given a requisition has active applications, when deletion is attempted, then the constraint prevents removal or requires an explicit cascade/close action; Given an offer is linked to an application, when that application is closed, then the linkage remains intact and consistent

title: Configure requisition and offer approver graphs
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure the approver graph and routing rules for requisition and offer approvals as governed metadata, so that approval routing can change without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage approval configuration, when I define an approver graph (levels, approvers by role/department, thresholds), then it is saved as versioned, effective-dated config that the workflow engine reads; Given approval thresholds are defined, when a requisition's attributes (headcount, grade, budget) meet a rule, then the config determines the required approval path; Given I publish a new version of the approver graph, when subsequent requisitions are submitted, then they follow the new routing while in-flight approvals retain the version they started under; Given a configuration change is saved, when it takes effect, then it is captured with author, version, and effective date
source: FR 6.13.1, FR 6.13.5 (L2 config — approver graphs / decision tables)

title: Define custom fields for requisitions and candidates
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define custom/UDF field schemas for requisitions, candidates, and applications, so that we can capture tenant-specific hiring data without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage field configuration, when I add a custom field (type, label, validation, mandatory flag) to a requisition or candidate schema, then it is saved as versioned metadata; Given custom fields are defined, when a user opens the corresponding form, then the dynamic fields render with their configured validation; Given a field is retired, when new records are created, then it no longer appears while historical records retain their captured values; Given custom-field config changes, when saved, then the change is effective-dated and auditable

title: Workflow engine executes configured hiring and approval workflows
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the shared workflow engine to execute the configured hiring and approval workflows, so that routing, stage transitions, and escalations behave exactly as defined in config.
acceptance_criteria: Given an approval or hiring workflow is configured, when a requisition or offer is submitted, then the workflow engine instantiates the process and routes it to the correct approver per the config; Given an approver acts or a stage completes, when the engine evaluates the next step, then it advances, branches, or escalates according to the configured rules; Given an approval is pending beyond its SLA, when the escalation rule fires, then the engine reassigns or notifies per config; Given a workflow instance runs, when any step executes, then the engine records the transition, actor, and timestamp for audit

title: Rules engine evaluates hiring stage-gating and eligibility
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the shared rules engine to evaluate stage-gating and eligibility decision tables, so that candidates only advance when configured conditions are met.
acceptance_criteria: Given stage-gating decision tables are configured, when a candidate is moved to a new stage, then the rules engine evaluates the entry conditions (e.g., required scorecards, completed reference checks) and permits or blocks the transition; Given offer eligibility rules exist, when an offer is initiated, then the engine evaluates the candidate/application against the decision table and returns eligible or the failing conditions; Given a rule set is updated in config, when the engine next evaluates, then it applies the current version without code deployment; Given the engine evaluates a rule, when a decision is returned, then the inputs, matched rule, and outcome are logged for traceability

title: Notification engine sends hiring event notifications from templates
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the shared notification engine to send templated notifications on hiring events, so that candidates, recruiters, hiring managers, and approvers are kept informed automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Given notification templates and triggers are configured, when a hiring event occurs (requisition approved, interview scheduled, offer sent, candidate rejected), then the notification engine renders the template with merged data and dispatches it to the configured recipients; Given a recipient has channel preferences, when a notification is sent, then the engine honors the configured channel (email/in-app); Given a template is versioned, when a notification is generated, then it uses the current template version and records the send; Given a notification fails to deliver, when the engine detects the failure, then it retries or flags per config and logs the outcome

title: Dynamic forms engine renders configured hiring forms
user_story: As an Employee (User) participating in hiring, I want requisition, application, and scorecard forms to be rendered dynamically from configured field and criteria metadata, so that I always see the correct fields and validations without waiting for a release.
acceptance_criteria: Given a form's field/criteria metadata is configured, when I open the requisition, application, or scorecard form, then the forms engine renders exactly the configured fields, criteria, and validation rules; Given a field is marked mandatory or has validation, when I submit, then the engine enforces the rule and blocks invalid input; Given custom fields were added in config, when I open the form, then they appear without any code change; Given the underlying config version changes, when I open the form, then the engine renders the current version while stored submissions retain the version they were captured under

title: Self-service candidate portal to apply and track status
user_story: As an Employee (User) acting as a candidate, I want a self-service portal to apply, upload my resume, and track my application status, so that I can manage my candidacy through a clear, guided interface.
acceptance_criteria: Given I access the candidate portal, when I apply to an open requisition, then a metadata-driven application form lets me submit my details and upload my resume; Given I have submitted an application, when I return to the portal, then I can view my current stage, scheduled interviews, and any pending actions; Given I receive an offer, when I open the portal, then I can review the offer letter and accept or reject it from the same interface; Given I use the portal on different devices, when the SPA loads, then screens render responsively and reflect the tenant's theming

title: Metadata-driven recruiter pipeline board with search and navigation
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven recruiter pipeline board with search, filtering, and navigation across requisitions and candidates, so that I can manage the hiring pipeline efficiently from the SPA.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the recruiter workspace, when the pipeline board loads, then requisitions and candidates render in configurable stage columns/grids driven by metadata; Given I search or filter by requisition, candidate, stage, recruiter, or status, then the board and grids update to match the criteria; Given I drag a candidate between stages or open a record, when the action completes, then the change persists and the view refreshes without a full reload; Given the tenant applies custom fields or theming, when the screens render, then columns, filters, and styling reflect that configuration

title: Bulk-upload resumes against a requisition
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to bulk-upload multiple resume files or an entire folder against a specific open requisition, so that I can rapidly ingest large candidate batches without adding candidates one at a time.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the bulk resume upload screen; when I select multiple files or a folder and choose the target requisition, then each file is queued showing name, path, size and progress; Given I submit the queue, when processing runs, then resumes are parsed and linked as candidates to the chosen requisition and per-file status updates; Given a file fails or is unsupported, when processing completes, then the failed rows are flagged with a reason while successful ones are imported; Given many requisitions exist, when I pick the upload target, then I can filter by request id, department, work location, position, vacancies and closing date

title: Manage job vacancies with lifecycle status tabs
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to view and manage job vacancies (distinct from requisitions) through lifecycle status tabs, so that I can track each vacancy from creation to closure separately from its parent requisition.
acceptance_criteria: Given approved requisitions generate vacancies, when I open the job vacancy list, then each vacancy shows vacancy code, RRF id, work location, employee class, number of vacancies, created and closing dates, assigned recruiters and status; Given vacancies span stages, when I click a status tab (New, Assigned, In Process, Closed, Withdrawn, All), then only vacancies in that state are listed with a matching count; Given a vacancy nears its closing date, when I sort by position closing date, then at-risk vacancies surface for prioritisation; Given a vacancy is withdrawn or closed, when its status is set, then it is excluded from active sourcing

title: Configure the vacancy-to-recruiter assignment method
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure how vacancies are assigned to recruiters (manual, round-robin, or workload-based), so that hiring load is distributed according to our chosen policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage recruitment configuration, when I select Manual and save, then an authorised user assigns each vacancy to a recruiter directly; Given I select Round robin and save, when new vacancies arrive, then the system distributes them sequentially to recruiters by functional location; Given I select Workload and save, when new vacancies arrive, then the system assigns them based on each recruiter's current open-vacancy capacity; Given the method is changed, when saved, then subsequent vacancy assignments follow the new method while existing assignments are unaffected
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Configuration > Vacancy Assignment Method

title: Post vacancies to external and internal channels
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to publish a vacancy as a job posting to one or more posting channels, so that open positions are advertised to the right candidate audiences.
acceptance_criteria: Given an open vacancy, when I add a new posting and select a posting channel, source, position level and work location, then a posting is created and listed with its posting mode and status; Given postings exist, when I open the postings list, then each shows posting source, vacancy, department, position level, work location, employee class, vacancies, dates, status and channel; Given no postings exist, when I open the screen, then a clear empty state is shown; Given a posting is created, when I refresh, then it appears immediately in the list

title: Configure posting channels
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define and maintain the posting channels available for advertising vacancies, so that recruiters can publish to approved destinations with the correct posting mode.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage recruitment configuration, when I add a new posting channel with name, channel, type, posting mode and applicability, then it is saved and listed; Given a channel exists, when I view or edit it, then I can review or update its name, channel, type, posting mode and applicability; Given a channel should no longer be used, when I deactivate it, then it is not selectable when creating a posting; Given channels change, when I refresh, then the list reflects the current set

title: Route job postings through posting-source approvers
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want job postings to be approved by configured posting-source approvers before they go live, so that only authorised advertisements are published externally.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage recruitment configuration, when I add a posting-source approver rule with posting mode, employee class, location and approver(s), then the rule is saved and listed; Given a posting is submitted, when it matches an approver rule, then it is routed to the configured approver(s) and cannot go live until approved; Given an approver acts, when they approve or reject the posting, then the decision, approver and timestamp are recorded and the posting status updates; Given rules change, when I edit a rule, then subsequent postings follow the updated routing
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Configuration > Posting Source Approvers

title: Organise the talent pool with folders and review status
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to organise talent-pool candidates into folders and track their review status, so that I can group candidates by role or campaign and prioritise screening.
acceptance_criteria: Given candidates exist in the talent pool, when I select one or more and assign them to folder(s), then the candidates are added to the chosen folders; Given folders exist, when I filter by one or more selected folders, then only candidates in those folders are shown; Given candidates need screening, when I use the review-status tabs (Not Reviewed, Reviewed, All), then only candidates in that review state are listed with a matching count; Given I have applied filters, when I click Reset, then all filters clear and the full candidate list is restored

title: Import job applications into the talent pool from a mailbox
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to connect an application mailbox and auto-import inbound applications into the talent pool, so that emailed resumes become searchable candidates without manual entry.
acceptance_criteria: Given I enable application email import, when I add email server settings (server, username, port, encryption type, checking interval, timeout), then the settings are saved and the mailbox is polled on the configured interval; Given emails are imported, when I set the delete-after-import option to Yes, then imported messages are removed from the server to keep the mailbox manageable; Given a candidate profile is not shortlisted, when the non-shortlist notification option is enabled, then the candidate is notified accordingly; Given import runs, when a message is processed, then the applicant and attached resume appear in the talent pool with source recorded
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Configuration > Talent Pool Email

title: Configure sequential interview rounds mapped to panels
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure the sequence of interview rounds and map each round to an interview panel, so that candidates progress through a defined, consistent interview structure.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage interview configuration, when I add an interview-rounds set and assign a panel to each round, then the sequence is saved and listed; Given rounds are configured, when I filter by employee class, location, department and position, then only the matching rounds configuration is shown; Given a configuration exists, when I view or edit it, then I can review or update its round-to-panel mappings and applicability; Given a round-to-panel mapping is in effect, when a candidate is scheduled, then interviews are drawn from the panel mapped to that round

title: Schedule mass interviews for a batch of candidates
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to schedule interviews for multiple shortlisted candidates in one action, so that I can arrange interviews efficiently across the pipeline instead of one candidate at a time.
acceptance_criteria: Given shortlisted or in-review candidates exist, when I select several and choose Schedule Mass Interviews, then interviews are scheduled for all selected candidates against their current round; Given a mass schedule is created, when participants check their calendars, then each candidate's interview appears with role, round and panel details; Given a panel member has a conflict, when the batch is scheduled, then the conflict is surfaced for that slot; Given the batch completes, when I refresh the pipeline, then each candidate's interview status reflects the scheduled round
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Hiring > Schedule Interview / In Review

title: Configure weighted, scored pre-interview questionnaires
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to build pre-interview questionnaires with per-question values, scores, mandatory flags and position-level weightage, so that candidate pre-screening is quantified and comparable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage interview configuration, when I add a pre-interview question with category, question type, field type and position/employee-class specificity, then it is saved and listed; Given a question needs scoring, when I set its value, score and mandatory flag, then responses are scored and required consistently; Given questions vary in importance, when I add weightage for position-level questions, then the weightage is stored per position level and applied to scoring; Given questions change, when I refresh, then the questionnaire list reflects the current configuration

title: Record per-round interview feedback as an interviewer
user_story: As an Employee (User) serving as an interviewer, I want to record structured feedback for each candidate against the specific interview round I conducted, so that decisions are based on documented, round-level assessments.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the interview feedback view, when it loads, then candidates with scheduled interviews and their round details are shown; Given I select a candidate whose interview I conducted, when I record feedback, then my assessment is captured against that candidate and round, attributed to me and timestamped; Given I need to find a candidate, when I search by name, then only matching candidates are listed; Given feedback is submitted, when the hiring manager reviews the candidate, then my round-level feedback is visible alongside other rounds

title: Place candidates on hold and resume them into the pipeline
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to place candidates on hold and later move them back into an active pipeline stage, so that I can pause progression without losing a candidate's history.
acceptance_criteria: Given a candidate's progression must pause, when I place them on hold, then they appear on the On Hold list with an On Hold interview status and full details; Given on-hold candidates exist, when I search by name, then only matching paused candidates are listed; Given a hold should end, when I switch the candidate to another stage filter (In-Review, Interview, Offered), then the candidate moves off hold into that stage; Given the on-hold list changes, when I refresh, then it reloads with the current paused candidates
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Hiring > On Hold Candidates

title: Configure location-based offer approvers with out-of-band salary approval
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure offer approvers by location and add an out-of-band salary approver, so that offers route to the right approver and above-band offers get extra sign-off.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage offer configuration, when I add an offer-approver rule selecting location(s), employee-class specificity and approver(s), then the rule is saved and listed; Given an offer exceeds the normal salary band, when it is submitted, then it additionally routes to the configured out-of-band salary approver before release; Given locations or approvers change, when I edit a rule, then the updated approval matrix applies to subsequent offers; Given rules exist, when I open the list, then each shows its location(s), employee-class specificity and approver(s)

title: Manage the full offer lifecycle: release, cancel, refuse
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want distinct offer lifecycle actions for releasing, cancelling and refusing offers with dedicated status views, so that every offer outcome is tracked precisely rather than as a single accept/reject flag.
acceptance_criteria: Given an approved offer is ready, when I release it from the Release Offer view, then the candidate moves to the offered stage and the offer is dispatched; Given a released or accepted offer must be withdrawn by the company, when I cancel it from the Cancel Offer view, then the candidate's offer is cancelled and the status is recorded; Given a candidate declines, when their refusal is captured in the Refuse Offer view, then the offer is marked refused with the candidate retained in history; Given a candidature is dropped, when it is cancelled, then the candidate appears in the Cancelled view with a Cancelled interview status; Given I work any of these views, when I search by name or switch stage filters, then the relevant candidates and counts are shown
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Hiring > Release Offer / Cancel Offer / Refuse Offer / Cancelled

title: Configure recruitment letter templates by letter type
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to maintain letter templates for offer, appointment, joining and cancel-offer letters, so that each hiring document is generated from an approved, correct template.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage recruitment configuration, when I open the letter templates list, then each template shows its template type id, letter type and description; Given a letter is to be issued, when the system generates it, then it uses the template matching the letter type (offer, appointment, joining, cancel offer); Given I need to inspect content, when I view a template, then its full content and merge fields are displayed; Given templates change, when I refresh, then the list reflects the current set of letter types

title: Issue joining letters to accepted hires
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to issue joining letters to candidates who have accepted their offer, so that confirmed hires receive their joining documentation before their start date.
acceptance_criteria: Given candidates have accepted offers, when I open the Joining Letter view, then candidates in the offer-accepted stage are shown with full details; Given a specific hire needs a joining letter, when I search by name, then only matching candidates are listed; Given a joining letter is generated, when it is issued, then it is produced from the joining-letter template with the candidate's details merged in; Given I work the view, when I switch hiring stage filters, then I can move between pipeline stages from the Joining Letter view

title: Issue appointment letters to joined hires
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to issue appointment letters to candidates who have joined, so that new employees receive formal appointment documentation once their status is Joined.
acceptance_criteria: Given candidates have joined, when I open the Appointment Letter view, then joined candidates (interview status Joined) are shown with full details; Given a hire needs an appointment letter, when I search by name, then only matching joined candidates are listed; Given an appointment letter is generated, when it is issued, then it is produced from the appointment-letter template for that hire; Given multiple appointment letters are pending, when the list loads, then the item range and count are shown so I can confirm how many remain

title: Run pre-onboarding checklists driven by a configurable questionnaire
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a pre-onboarding checklist per candidate driven by a configurable checklist questionnaire, so that pre-joining tasks are captured and tracked before a new hire starts.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage recruitment configuration, when I add checklist questions with response-from, question type, field type, display type, value and mandatory flag, then they form the pre-joining checklist; Given a candidate is pre-joining, when I open their pre-onboarding checklist, then the configured checklist items are presented for completion; Given mandatory items exist, when the checklist is submitted, then required responses must be provided before completion; Given the checklist screen is requested, when it loads, then it opens within an acceptable time without a navigation timeout
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Pre Onboarding Checklist / Configuration > CheckList Questionnaire

title: Configure resource-requisition approvers including non-budgeted positions
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure requisition approvers by template, employee class and applicability, with a distinct approver for non-budgeted positions, so that unbudgeted hiring requests receive the additional approval they require.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage recruitment configuration, when I add a resource-requisition approver rule with template, employee class and approver(s), then the rule is saved and listed; Given a requisition is for a non-budgeted position, when it is submitted, then it additionally routes to the configured non-budgeted-position approver(s); Given many rules exist, when I filter by employee class, location, department and position, then only matching rules are shown; Given a rule exists, when I view or edit it, then I can review or update its template name, approver(s) and applicability
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Configuration > Resource Requisition Approvers

title: Configure pre-joining HR and network approvers
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure separate pre-joining approvers for HR tasks and for IT/network tasks, so that each type of pre-joining task is routed to the correct approver.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage recruitment configuration, when I add a pre-joining HR approver, then HR-side pre-joining tasks are routed to that approver and the approver is listed; Given IT provisioning must be approved, when I add a pre-joining network approver, then network/IT pre-joining tasks are routed to that approver; Given approvers change, when I edit an HR or network approver, then the update is saved and applied to subsequent pre-joining tasks; Given approvers exist, when I open each list, then the configured approvers are shown
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Configuration > Pre Joining HR Approvers / Pre Joining Network Approvers

title: Require candidate documents at specific recruitment stages
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define which documents candidates must submit at each recruitment stage, so that the right documents are collected at the right point in the process.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage recruitment configuration, when I enable stage-based required documents, then document setup becomes available; Given setup is enabled, when I add a required document with name, type and required-at stage, then it is saved and listed; Given rules change, when I edit a required document, then the updated document rule is saved; Given documents exist, when I open the list, then each shows document name, document type, employee-class specificity and required-at stage
source: Kensium: Recruitment > Configuration > Required Candidate Documents

title: Configure data carry-over for rehired candidates
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure which fields and sections carry over when a former employee is rehired, so that returning employees' data is reused correctly instead of re-entered.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage rehire configuration, when I select fields on the Personal Details and Contact Details tabs and save, then those fields are set to carry over on rehire; Given professional history should be retained, when I configure the Academic Details, Work Experience and Skills tabs, then those sections carry over on rehire; Given onboarding steps differ for returnees, when I configure the Employee Verification and Orientation tabs, then those steps are set for rehire; Given I complete a tab, when I click Save and Next, then the settings are saved and the next tab opens

title: Profile recruiter strengths for vacancy matching
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to record each recruiter's strengths and applicability, so that vacancies can be matched to recruiters best suited to fill them.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage recruitment configuration, when I add recruiter strengths with recruiter name, strengths and applicability, then the entry is saved and listed; Given strengths exist, when I open the list, then each entry shows recruiter name, strengths and applicability; Given a recruiter's profile changes, when I update the entry, then the revised strengths are saved; Given assignment considers fit, when vacancies are matched, then recruiter strengths inform which recruiter is best suited
